title: New Ebola Outbreak Causes Concern in the Democratic Republic of Congo
subtitle: Health officials work to contain a rare strain of the virus as cases spread across borders.

summary.en: A new Ebola outbreak has emerged in the Democratic Republic of Congo, resulting in 87 deaths so far. The virus has been identified in the Ituri province, with hundreds of suspected cases reported. Health experts are particularly worried because this specific strain lacks an approved vaccine. One case has already been confirmed in neighboring Uganda, raising fears about regional spread. International health organizations are now coordinating efforts to improve surveillance and protective measures.
summary.zh: 剛果民主共和國爆發新一波伊波拉疫情，迄今已造成八十七人死亡。病毒已在伊圖里省（Ituri）被發現，並通報了數百起疑似病例。衛生專家特別擔憂，因為此特定病毒株目前尚無核准的疫苗。鄰國烏干達已確認一起病例，引發對區域擴散的恐懼。國際衛生組織目前正協調各方努力，以加強監測與防護措施。

paragraphs.en: Health officials in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are facing a serious public health challenge as a new Ebola outbreak spreads through the country. According to NPR News, the outbreak began in late April and has already caused 87 deaths. There are currently 336 suspected cases, mostly centered in the mining towns of Mongwalu and Rwampara. These areas are described as vulnerable and fragile, with many workers traveling in and out, which makes controlling the movement of the virus difficult.
paragraphs.zh: 剛果民主共和國（Democratic Republic of Congo, DRC）的衛生官員正面臨嚴峻的公共衛生挑戰，因為該國正爆發新一波伊波拉疫情。根據美國國家公共廣播電台（NPR）報導，這波疫情始於四月下旬，目前已造成八十七人死亡。現有三百三十六起疑似病例，主要集中在蒙瓦魯（Mongwalu）與魯安帕拉（Rwampara）等礦業城鎮。這些地區被形容為脆弱且不穩定，由於許多工人頻繁進出，導致難以控管病毒的傳播。

paragraphs.en: Ebola is a dangerous disease that spreads through contact with blood or other bodily fluids, as well as contaminated surfaces. Common symptoms include fever, body pain, weakness, and vomiting. In some severe cases, patients may also experience bleeding. Because the virus is highly transmissible—meaning it spreads easily from person to person—experts strongly recommend that healthcare workers use protective gear. This includes masks, gloves, gowns, goggles, and head coverings to prevent infection.
paragraphs.zh: 伊波拉是一種危險的疾病，透過接觸血液或其他體液，以及受污染的表面進行傳播。常見症狀包括發燒、身體疼痛、虛弱與嘔吐。在某些嚴重病例中，患者可能會出現出血現象。由於該病毒傳染性極高，意即極易在人與人之間擴散，專家強烈建議醫療人員使用防護裝備，包括口罩、手套、防護衣、護目鏡與頭罩，以預防感染。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Jean Kaseya, the director general of the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention, noted that this specific outbreak involves the Bundibugyo strain of the virus. Unlike the more common Zaire strain, this version is less understood by scientists. Most importantly, there is currently no approved vaccine available to protect the public. While researchers are studying an experimental vaccine that has shown some promise in tests on monkeys, it has not yet been fully tested or proven effective for human patients.
paragraphs.zh: 非洲疾病管制暨預防中心（Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention）總幹事卡塞亞（Jean Kaseya）博士指出，此次疫情涉及的是該病毒的邦迪布焦（Bundibugyo）株。與較常見的薩伊（Zaire）株不同，科學界對此版本的病毒了解較少。最重要的是，目前尚無核准的疫苗可供民眾接種。儘管研究人員正在研發一種在猴子實驗中展現潛力的實驗性疫苗，但該疫苗尚未經過完整測試，也未證實對人類患者有效。

paragraphs.en: The situation is made more complex by the movement of people across borders. NPR News reported that a 59-year-old man from the DRC traveled to Kampala, the capital of Uganda, while he was already sick. He used public transportation and visited a hospital before he passed away. His body was later taken back across the border for burial. This event highlights the difficulty of tracking the virus, as the man had contact with many people during his journey.
paragraphs.zh: 人口跨國流動使情況變得更加複雜。NPR 報導指出，一名來自剛果民主共和國的五十九歲男子在患病期間前往烏干達首都坎帕拉（Kampala）。他在過世前曾搭乘大眾運輸工具並前往醫院就診，遺體隨後被運回邊境安葬。此事件凸顯了追蹤病毒的困難，因為該名男子在旅途中曾與許多人接觸。

paragraphs.en: Health experts are concerned that the outbreak may be larger than current data suggests. Dr. Craig Spencer, a professor at the Brown University of Public Health, noted on social media that the fact that officials are only now recognizing the scale of the outbreak means the virus has likely been spreading for some time without being noticed. This delay makes the task of stopping the virus much harder for local authorities and international partners.
paragraphs.zh: 衛生專家擔心疫情規模可能比現有數據顯示的更大。布朗大學公共衛生學院（Brown University of Public Health）教授史賓賽（Craig Spencer）博士在社群媒體上表示，官員直到現在才意識到疫情規模，意味著病毒很可能已在未被察覺的情況下傳播了一段時間。這種延遲使得地方當局與國際合作夥伴遏止病毒的任務變得更加艱鉅。

paragraphs.en: To address these challenges, the Africa CDC is working with various nonprofits and health organizations to improve cross-border surveillance and emergency response efforts. One major hurdle is the lack of local manufacturing for personal protective equipment (PPE). Dr. Kaseya explained that his team is actively seeking funds to solve this problem, as protective gear is essential to keep medical workers safe while they treat the sick.
paragraphs.zh: 為因應這些挑戰，非洲疾病管制暨預防中心正與各類非營利組織及衛生機構合作，以改善跨國監測與緊急應變工作。一大障礙是當地缺乏個人防護裝備（PPE）的製造能力。卡塞亞博士解釋，他的團隊正積極尋求資金以解決此問題，因為防護裝備對於確保醫護人員在治療病患時的安全至關重要。

paragraphs.en: As the situation continues to develop, the focus remains on identifying new cases quickly and preventing further transmission. The combination of a rare, poorly understood virus strain and a region with high human movement presents a significant test for health systems in the area. Officials are urging people to be cautious and are working to ensure that communities have the resources needed to manage the threat. For now, the global health community continues to watch the region closely, hoping to prevent the outbreak from growing into a larger crisis.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著情勢持續發展，重點仍放在快速識別新病例並防止進一步傳播。罕見且研究不足的病毒株，加上該地區人口流動頻繁，對當地的衛生系統構成了重大考驗。官員呼籲民眾保持謹慎，並致力於確保社區擁有應對威脅所需的資源。目前，全球衛生界持續密切關注該地區，希望能防止疫情擴大成為更嚴重的危機。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Located or concentrated in a specific place.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（中心），這裡作動詞的過去分詞，用來描述疫情集中的地點。文中：There are currently 336 suspected cases, mostly centered in the mining towns of Mongwalu and Rwampara.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A polite or euphemistic way to say someone has died.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是「die」的委婉說法，在新聞報導中常被用來表示對死者的尊重。文中：He used public transportation and visited a hospital before he passed away.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A problem or difficulty that must be overcome.
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意指田徑比賽中的「跨欄」，這裡引申為解決問題過程中的困難。文中：One major hurdle is the lack of local manufacturing for personal protective equipment (PPE).

keyTerms.definitionEn: A difficult situation that shows how strong or capable someone or something is.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（考試、測試），這裡指對醫療系統能力的嚴峻考驗。文中：The combination of a rare, poorly understood virus strain and a region with high human movement presents a significant test for health systems in the area.

sources.name: NPR News
sources.title: A new Ebola outbreak has already killed 87 people in Democratic Republic of Congo

quiz.question: What is the primary reason given for why the current Ebola outbreak in the DRC is particularly difficult to control?

options.A: The symptoms of the virus are too mild for people to notice until it is too late.
options.B: The outbreak is centered in mining towns where there is a high level of population mobility.
options.C: The virus is primarily spreading through contaminated water sources in major cities.
options.D: Local healthcare workers refuse to use the required protective gear due to cultural beliefs.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第一段明確指出，疫情主要集中在 Mongwalu 和 Rwampara 等採礦城鎮，這些地區人口流動性高（workers traveling in and out），使得控制病毒傳播變得困難。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到症狀包括發燒、疼痛與嘔吐，並非輕微；選項 C 錯誤，文中提到傳播途徑是體液與接觸表面，並未提及水資源；選項 D 在文中完全沒有提到，屬於無中生有的錯誤選項。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the potential for containing the current Ebola outbreak?

options.A: The outbreak will likely end soon because a new vaccine has been successfully tested on humans.
options.B: The virus is expected to remain contained within the mining towns of the DRC.
options.C: The lack of local medical resources and the delay in detection make a rapid resolution unlikely.
options.D: The virus is less dangerous than previous outbreaks because it is the Bundibugyo strain.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第五段與第六段，專家指出疫情被發現的時間點太晚，且缺乏在地生產的個人防護裝備（PPE），這些因素加總起來使得控制疫情的任務變得更加艱鉅。選項 A 錯誤，因為疫苗尚未經人體測試證明有效；選項 B 錯誤，文中提到病患已跨越國界前往烏干達，顯示疫情已擴散；選項 D 錯誤，文章僅提到此菌株較少被研究，並未說它比較不危險。

quiz.question: In the sixth paragraph, what does the word 'hurdle' mean as it is used in the context of the Africa CDC's efforts?

options.A: An obstacle or challenge that prevents progress.
options.B: A specific type of medical equipment used for protection.
options.C: A financial grant provided by international organizations.
options.D: A physical barrier built along the border to stop movement.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在第六段中，作者提到「One major hurdle is the lack of local manufacturing」，意指缺乏在地製造能力是一個阻礙或困難，影響了防疫工作的進展。選項 B 錯誤，hurdle 並非醫療設備；選項 C 錯誤，文中提到的是正在尋求資金（seeking funds），而非已經獲得資金；選項 D 錯誤，這是在比喻防疫過程中的困難，而非實體的國界圍欄。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the Ebola outbreak in the DRC?

options.A: The Ebola virus has evolved to become a permanent fixture in the mining towns of Africa.
options.B: International health organizations are solely responsible for the failure to stop the virus.
options.C: The combination of a rare virus strain and high human mobility creates a complex public health crisis.
options.D: The most effective way to stop Ebola is to close all international borders immediately.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。這篇文章的核心在於說明目前 DRC 的疫情為何棘手：除了病毒株較為罕見且缺乏疫苗外，加上人口高度流動與防疫資源不足，導致這場公共衛生危機難以處理。選項 A 過度推論，文章未稱其為「永久性」；選項 B 錯誤，文章並未將責任全歸咎於單一組織；選項 D 錯誤，文中提到的是加強邊境監控，而非全面關閉邊境，該選項非文章主旨。
